__classcell__r
Timeouta>
    Abstraction for timeout operations. Using time.monotonic() if available
    or time.time() in all other cases.
    The class can also be initialized with 0 or None, in order to support
    non-blocking and fully blocking I/O operations. The attributes
    is_non_blocking and is_infinite are set accordingly.
    
	monotonicc
z(Initialize a timeout with given durationNr
is_infinite
is_non_blocking
duration
TIME
target_time
Timeout.__init__c
z4Return a boolean, telling if the timeout has expiredNr
	time_leftr-
expired
Timeout.expiredc
z:Return how many seconds are left until the timeout expiresr
max)
deltar
Timeout.time_leftc
zb        Restart a timeout, only supported if a timeout was already set up
        before.
        N)
restart

SerialBasezh    Serial port base class. Provides __init__ function and properties to
    get/set port settings.
    )
        Initialize comm port object. If a "port" is given, then the port will be
        opened immediately. Otherwise a Serial port object in closed state
        is returned.
        FNT

z{        Get the current port setting. The value that was passed on init or using
        setPort() is passed back.
        )
SerialBase.portc
z!        Change the port.
        Nz'"port" must be None or a string, not {})
basestringrj

was_openr
z"Get the current baud rate setting.)
SerialBase.baudratec
        Change baud rate. It raises a ValueError if the port is open and the
        baud rate is not possible. If the port is closed, then the value is
        accepted and the exception is raised when the port is opened.
        z
Not a valid baudrate: {!r}r
intr
_reconfigure_port)

        values will simply left unchanged.
